title: 7-command wiki skill architecture
description: ingest (data->entries), absorb (entries->wiki articles), query (read-only wiki search), cleanup (audit+enrich), breakdown (find+create missing articles), rebuild-index, reorganize. Each command has distinct phases and rules.

title: Anti-cramming / anti-thinning balance
description: Anti-cramming: if adding a third paragraph about a sub-topic, split into its own page. Anti-thinning: creating a page isn't the win, enriching it is. A stub when 4 entries mention the topic is a failure. Balance breadth with depth.

title: 15-entry checkpoint cycle
description: Every 15 absorbed entries: rebuild index, audit new article count (zero = cramming), quality audit top 3 articles (coherent story vs event log?), check line counts, restructure directories. Systematic maintenance rhythm.

title: Parallel subagent cleanup workflow
description: Phase 1: build context (read all). Phase 2: spawn batches of 5 subagents, each audits one article (structure, line count, tone, quote density, narrative coherence, wikilinks). Phase 3: deduplicate, create new articles, fix links, rebuild indices.

title: Directory taxonomy with emergent categories
description: 30+ directory types across 6 groups: Core (people/projects/places/events), Media (books/films/music/tools), Inner Life (philosophies/patterns/tensions), Narrative (eras/transitions/decisions), Relationships, Work (strategies/techniques/skills). Directories emerge from data, not pre-created.

title: Writer not filing clerk identity
description: Core identity framing: 'You are a writer compiling a personal knowledge wiki, not a filing clerk.' The question is never 'where do I put this fact?' but 'what does this mean, and how does it connect to what I already know?' This shapes every operation.

title: Writing standards with Steve Jobs test
description: Golden rule: 'This is not Wikipedia about the thing. This is about the thing's role in the subject's life.' Wikipedia tone (flat, factual), no AI voice, max 2 quotes per article, length targets (15-100 lines by type). Steve Jobs test for structure: eras not events.

title: Concurrency safety rules
description: Never delete/overwrite without reading first. Re-read articles immediately before editing. Never modify _absorb_log.json. Rebuild indices only at end of commands. Practical wisdom for LLM file manipulation.

title: Concept articles as mind-mapping
description: When same theme surfaces across entries (loneliness, creative philosophy, recovery from burnout), that pattern deserves its own article. Concept articles are where wiki becomes a map of a mind instead of a contact list. Most valuable article type.

title: No session continuity or compaction handling
description: Assumes single long-running session. No treatment of how to resume absorb after context reset, no state persistence beyond _absorb_log.json, no checkpoint/restore pattern for multi-session work.

title: No versioning or schema evolution
description: No schema_version field, no migration path if taxonomy changes, no handling of breaking changes to article format. Compare to our v2.0 extraction-journal evolution.
